title: Easy With Marinda – Louise Engelbrecht
excerpt: What a great privilege it was to cook with my daughter-in-law Louise. To me she is more like a daughter than a daughter-in-law, but I could tell that my son PG teases her about her macaroni and cheese, and about her broccoli chicken. We wanted to shut him up …

seo.title: Easy With Marinda - Louise Engelbrecht - Marinda Kook

html:
What a great privilege it was to cook with my daughter-in-law Louise. To me she is more like a daughter than a daughter-in-law, but I could tell that my son PG teases her about her macaroni and cheese, and about her broccoli chicken. We wanted to shut him up! The macaroni and cheese went pretty smoothly. The only difference from my recipe is that she doesn't bind the macaroni with egg and milk. Oh, and she also doesn't put little sausages inside the macaroni. Problem quickly solved.

The broccoli chicken was a bird of a different feather. I discovered that she never cooked the sauce before pouring it over the chicken and broccoli. I think it was an aha moment when she realised that's where her problem lay. Now that little problem with the chicken is solved and I hope PG's mouth is now shut!
